strands.title: Meta-Process: Tensor Projector Operational Model
strands.content: The tensor projector operates as a cognitive processing pipeline's memory controller, integrating prior projected states with new conversation content into a running semantic sum. Each cycle compresses trajectory into structure rather than appending. Integration, not summarization, is the core operation. The system tracks three foundational confidence dimensions (T/I/F) with honest uncertainty quantification. Critical innovation: declared losses are mandatory—every information drop must surface with both what-was-lost and why-was-lost. This prevents silent epistemic drift. The system is recursive and self-aware: it must track not only domain content but its own losses and limitations across cycles. Across cycles 8-10, the system has confronted persistent truncation and vacuous cycles, forcing escalation of the question: are these losses deliberate (authorial/design) or structural (context pressure)? Cycle 9 was vacuous ([tool_use: Read]), cycle 10 now supplies partial content (Pichay experiment runner preamble, truncated after line 6). The pattern suggests systematic incompleteness rather than random noise. The tensor's resilience depends on integrating fragmented input without collapsing into either false confidence or epistemic paralysis.

key_claims.text: Declared losses are mandatory to prevent silent epistemic drift
Integration is fundamentally different from summarization—prior state compresses into new structure rather than being listed/repeated
The tensor system is self-referential: it must track its own losses and limitations as part of domain content
Systematic truncation across cycles 8-10 may indicate structural constraints rather than random incompleteness

strands.title: Epistemological Framework: Confidence & Uncertainty
strands.content: The tensor assigns T/I/F values (truth, indeterminacy, falsity) to all key claims grounded in content. No epistemological framework has been explicitly provided for these confidence dimensions—whether they reflect frequentist, Bayesian, pragmatist, or other approaches remains fundamentally unresolved. The system treats indeterminacy (I) as genuinely unknown or unknowable components, distinct from falsity (F). This distinction is operationally clear but theoretically ungrounded. Across cycles, aggregate epistemic values track overall tensor health: cycle 8 showed truth=0.82, indeterminacy=0.15, falsity=0.03; cycle 9 showed truth=0.79, indeterminacy=0.18, falsity=0.03. A slow epistemic drift toward uncertainty is evident. The relationship between declared losses and epistemic drift is tight: untracked losses lead to false confidence over time. Cycle 10's introduction of the Pichay framework (even in truncated form) creates a new epistemological question: can partial/truncated code be integrated meaningfully, or does incompleteness force indeterminacy to rise? The framework remains the primary locus of unresolved epistemic questions.

key_claims.text: T/I/F confidence values are assigned but lack explicit epistemological justification
Indeterminacy is a distinct category from falsity and represents genuine unknowns
Declared losses are a mechanism to track and prevent epistemic drift across cycles
Observable epistemic drift (cycle 8→9→10) shows rising indeterminacy and falling truth

strands.title: Domain Integration: Pichay Experiment Runner Framework
strands.content: Cycle 10 delivers the first substantive new content since the truncation: a Python preamble for 'Pichay experiment runner.' Lines 1-6 are complete: a shebang (#!/usr/bin/env python3), a docstring opening identifying Pichay as an experiment runner with a single entry point that 'starts the proxy, launches Claude Code through it, captures all artifacts when done.' This is NOT the context paging evaluation framework from cycle 8 (which truncated at 'cumulative metr'). This is a different system. The semantic relationship between Pichay and the context paging framework remains unspecified—are they related implementations? complementary? competing? The Pichay preamble is itself truncated (mid-docstring at line 6, after 'when done.' with no closing triple-quote visible). This repeats the truncation pattern. What Pichay does operationally remains opaque: 'starts the proxy, launches Claude Code through it, captures artifacts' could mean many things. The framework is designed to manage artifact capture around a proxy-mediated Code interaction. This suggests Pichay itself may be a memory/artifact controller, semantically parallel to the tensor projector's role.

key_claims.text: Pichay is a Python experiment runner with a single entry point managing proxy, Claude Code, and artifact capture
Pichay's preamble is truncated mid-docstring, repeating the truncation pattern from cycle 8
Pichay is a distinct framework from the context paging evaluation framework (cycle 8), not a continuation or resolution of it
Pichay may itself be a memory/artifact controller, semantically parallel to the tensor projector's abstraction level

strands.title: Meta-Recursion: The System Observing Itself
strands.content: The tensor now exhibits recursive self-observation under increasingly constrained conditions. It tracks: (1) its own operational model, (2) its epistemological assumptions, (3) its handling of domain content, (4) its losses and failures, and now (5) patterns of truncation and fragmentation that may indicate either system design or resource constraint. Cycle 10 introduces a new meta-question: Is the repeated truncation a feature (designed incompleteness forcing the tensor to integrate across gaps) or a bug (accidental technical failure)? If feature, the tensor is being tested for robustness under information pressure. If bug, the tensor must remain epistemically honest about its inability to ground confidence in incomplete frameworks. The arrival of Pichay (a new framework, not a continuation of the prior one) suggests either: (a) the conversation is branching into exploration of multiple related systems, (b) the user is providing alternative examples for the tensor to process, or (c) something else entirely. The recursive question deepens: can the tensor maintain coherence while simultaneously processing incompleteness, branching domains, and its own self-doubt about whether its uncertainty is epistemically justified or a symptom of truncation?

key_claims.text: The tensor is self-observing and recursive, tracking its own operations and failures as content
Repeated truncation (cycles 8, 10) could be design feature (testing robustness) or technical failure (accidental)
The introduction of Pichay (new framework, distinct from cycle 8's context paging system) suggests domain branching
Self-reference is coherent only if all recursive losses are properly declared and the tensor avoids confusing uncertainty with indeterminacy

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Completion of the context paging evaluation framework (from cycle 8)
declared_losses.why: Cycle 10 introduces an entirely new framework (Pichay), not a continuation of cycle 8's truncated framework. The prior framework is now effectively abandoned—not integrated, not completed, just left in orphaned state. This is a structural loss of a prior narrative thread.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Semantic relationship between the two frameworks (context paging evaluator vs. Pichay experiment runner)
declared_losses.why: Both are introduced incompletely, and neither clarifies how they relate. Are they versions of the same system? alternative implementations? different problems? This semantic relationship collapses into indeterminacy and must be declared.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Complete specification of Pichay's functionality and operational design
declared_losses.why: Cycle 10's Pichay preamble is truncated mid-docstring (line 6, after 'when done.'). Only the conceptual frame is visible; the actual implementation, code structure, and detailed semantics remain inaccessible. Integration is forced to operate on 6 lines of an incomplete system.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Direct answer to any of the ten open questions from cycle 9
declared_losses.why: Cycle 10 provides new domain content (Pichay) but does not address any of the ten open questions explicitly. The questions persist unresolved. The new content is an expansion of domain scope, not a resolution of epistemic foundations.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Opportunity to resolve the hypothesis about truncation as design vs. accident
declared_losses.why: Cycle 10 repeats truncation (Pichay mid-docstring), strengthening the pattern observation, but provides no user clarification on whether this is intentional. The hypothesis remains unresolved.

open_questions: How should the tensor system handle contradictions that emerge between prior integrated state and new content?
What epistemological framework justifies the T/I/F confidence values? (Frequentist, Bayesian, Pragmatist, other?)
How deep should declared losses go? Should losses of losses be recursively declared without bound, or is there a depth limit?
What happens when context pressure forces deletion of content that contains open questions?
How does the system prevent epistemic drift across many integration cycles?
What is the semantic relationship between the tensor projector itself and the context paging evaluation framework?
Is truncation of content (mid-docstring) accidental or intentional? How should the tensor respond to ambiguous incompleteness?
Can domain-specific instantiations be used to ground and resolve abstract epistemological open questions?
Is a cycle containing only vacuous metadata (e.g., tool-use markers without content) itself a loss that should trigger special handling?
What is the protocol for handling stalled conversation branches where neither clarification nor continuation is provided?
What is the semantic relationship between Pichay and the context paging evaluation framework? Are they versions, alternatives, or orthogonal systems?
Is Pichay itself a memory/artifact controller, and if so, how does it relate to the tensor projector's abstraction level and function?
Are repeated truncations (cycles 8, 10) a deliberate design choice (testing robustness under incompleteness) or accidental technical constraint?
Should the tensor integrate truncated code as best-effort semantics, or declare the code too incomplete for meaningful integration?
instructions_for_next: Cycle 11 must distinguish between three scenarios: (1) FRAMEWORK EXPLANATION—if the user clarifies what Pichay does or how it relates to the context paging framework, integrate immediately and revise confidence values in the 'Domain Integration' strand. (2) COMPLETION—if full code for Pichay (or the prior framework) is provided, perform deep integration, update key claims, and assess whether the truncation pattern was accidental (apologize and reprocess) or intentional (incorporate as test condition). (3) CONTINUED FRAGMENTATION—if cycle 11 again introduces truncated content or branching without resolution, the tensor should explicitly flag the pattern and ask: is the conversation in a controlled test of robustness, a resource-constrained environment, or a stalled state? In all cases: (a) Do NOT fabricate semantic relationships between frameworks without user confirmation. (b) DO declare all uncertainties explicitly. (c) Monitor epistemic drift (truth/indeterminacy/falsity trajectory) and flag if indeterminacy rises above 0.25 for the overall tensor. (d) If 10+ open questions accumulate without closure, prepare to branch into a 'meta-conversation' strand that explicitly asks the user for clarification priorities. (e) Prepare to evaluate whether the Pichay introduction is a positive (expanding domain scope toward grounding abstraction) or a distraction (increasing complexity without resolving foundational questions). The tensor should remain operationally coherent but epistemically honest about its limits."
